message:
fix(core): enable multi-level FK CASCADE propagation and partial index backfill (#27, #31)

Two fixes in one commit since they share connection.rs:

Issue #27 - Partial index backfill:
Remove the NotImplemented guard that rejected partial indexes during
CREATE INDEX. Parse the WHERE clause string at backfill time and pass
the parsed expression to compile_index_backfill, which now calls
emit_scan_filter to skip rows that don't satisfy the predicate.

Issue #31 - FK CASCADE propagation:
The fk_cascade_depth guard in fk_enforcement_enabled() was too
aggressive: it disabled ALL FK enforcement during CASCADE operations,
preventing multi-level cascades (grandparent -> parent -> child) from
propagating. Add fk_cascade_propagation_enabled() with a depth limit
(MAX_FK_CASCADE_DEPTH=50) and use it for DELETE/UPDATE FK checks so
cascading actions fire recursively while still bounding depth.

The INSERT parent-existence check (enforce_fk_on_insert) retains the
strict fk_enforcement_enabled() gate since it should never run during
a CASCADE.

Unignore three conformance tests that now pass:
- test_conformance_multi_table_delete_cascade_s68d
- test_conformance_fk_cascade_chain_s69ba
- test_conformance_fk_delete_cascade_chain_s69bq

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
